---
title: "Yuneta Simplified"
subtitle: "An Asynchronous Development Framework"
page-layout: full
---

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](_static/yuneta-image.svg){width=200}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}

`Yuneta Simplified` is a **development framework** focused on **messaging** and
**services**, based on
[Event-driven](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Event-driven_programming),
[Automata-based](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Automata-based_programming) and
[Object-oriented](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Object-oriented_programming)
programming paradigms. Heavy use of JSON, **time-series**, **key-value**,
**flat-files** and **graph** concepts.

[Yuneta Simplified](https://yuneta.io) is a **real-time system** (RTS) that
includes **development**, **testing**, and **deployment** features. Built for
Linux and **deployable** on any **bare-metal** server.

Specialised in IoT data collection, device integration, and data exchange
and protocol adaptation between systems — including collection,
**publication/subscription**, and querying of **messages** in **real time**,
with **historical** data storage.

Messages (encrypted or plain text) circulating within the Yuneta system can be
persistent on disk or exist only while in transit or in memory. All data in
JSON.

For [Linux](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Linux). Versions in C, partially in
JavaScript.

:::

::::

---

## About this pilot {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-note}
This site is a **pilot migration** of the original Sphinx documentation at
`docs/doc.yuneta.io/` to [Quarto](https://quarto.org). It does **not** contain
all the pages — only a representative slice is included so we can compare
both stacks side by side before committing to a full migration.

**What's included**

- The **Philosophy** section (prose pages).
- The **istream** helper API (17 reference pages — the mechanical case).
- A **demo executable notebook** to validate Quarto's Jupyter integration.

**What's missing vs. the Sphinx site**

- Interactive execution via `sphinx_thebe` (no 1-to-1 in Quarto).
- `ablog` blog posts (Quarto has its own `listing:` format).
- `intersphinx` cross-references to Python stdlib.
- The full API reference (~800 pages) — only `istream` is ported.
:::

## Explore the pilot {.unnumbered}

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### [Philosophy](philosophy/philosophy.qmd)
The design principles behind Yuneta: events, actions, hierarchy, time and
data.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### [istream API](api/helpers/istream/index.qmd)
Input-stream helper: lifecycle, reading primitives, buffer inspection.
Seventeen function reference pages migrated mechanically by
`tools/docs-migration/myst_to_quarto.py`.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### [Demo notebook](notebooks/demo_timeseries.qmd)
A small executable Quarto document with a Python code cell — the
`myst_nb`-equivalent feature that made the stack choice relevant in the
first place.
:::

::::
